key_insight: Higher spray pressure in cold-sprayed copper drives particle velocity past the critical bonding threshold, improving ductility and conductivity while lowering hardness through recovery/recrystallisation.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies a high-pressure operating window for CSAM copper that better balances mechanical integrity and electrical performance for functional metal components.
method: Cold-sprayed copper on a LightSPEE3D system at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar.
Measured particle velocity, XRD microstructure, hardness, conductivity, tensile behavior, and SEM fracture/morphology.
Compared pressure-dependent process–structure–property trends to identify an optimum window.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports pressure-linked XRD crystallite refinement/dislocation changes, rising conductivity, and a clear strength-to-ductility transition with fully ductile fracture at higher pressures.
watch_item: Higher-pressure settings may improve performance, but process tuning must avoid over-softening from excessive recovery/recrystallisation and should be validated for part geometry and production scale.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
